Closes bp6-3d1.1, bp6-3d1.2, bp6-3d1.3, bp6-3d1.4, bp6-3d1.5

commit.message:
Fix CDN cache — extend to 10min to match cron interval

SQLite on Vercel /tmp is ephemeral per function instance. The CDN
cache must outlast the cron interval (10min) so users always get
cached data from the last cron run, not empty cold-start responses.

- s-maxage=600 (10min CDN cache, matches cron interval)
- stale-while-revalidate=60 (serve stale while revalidating)
- Error responses cached only 10s to recover quickly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): add dedup gate at GHA council entry to stop dispatch loop (#1514)

## Summary
- Added council:ticket-* label check at the START of the GHA council job
(before running Claude)
- Gated Slack notification and job summary behind dedup check
- Prevents notification spam when multiple Linear webhooks fire for the
same ticket

## Root cause
Linear sends webhooks for ALL issue updates (labels, sortOrder,
comments), not just state changes. Each webhook with `state.name == "In
Progress"` passes the n8n filter and dispatches a new GHA run. The
previous fix (n8n dedup + pr-hygiene) was insufficient because the
`council:ticket-*` label is added AFTER the dispatch, creating a race
window.

The concurrency group `claude-linear-$ticket_id` with
`cancel-in-progress: false` queues runs serially, so:
1. 1st run: no label → council runs → adds label → queues to workers  
2. 2nd+ runs: sees label → skips everything (no Slack, no council, no
queue)

## Test plan
- [ ] Dispatch a ticket that already has `council:ticket-go` label →
should skip with notice
- [ ] Dispatch a new ticket → should run council normally
- [ ] Verify no duplicate Slack messages for same ticket

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Multilingual AI parsing with action suggestions for Null by Input

The AI now reads full freetext messages (Danish, Swedish, Norwegian,
English) per line, extracts the customer name, fuzzy-matches it, and
proposes one of three actions: permanently_closed, nulled, or
add_comment — based on Scandinavian keywords and context.

Apply executes all three actions: seasonal null overrides, permanent
DB close, or customer_comments insert/update per season.

fix: payment_received handler sends Zelle address for prepay with pending OOS

For prepay clients with unresolved OOS flow (pending_oos_resolution in
conversation state), send oos_agrees template with Zelle address instead
of payment_received template with tracking. The client may not have
received proper payment instructions from the OOS handler LLM fallback.

Also clear pending_oos_resolution when oos_agrees template is sent in
OOS handler, so subsequent payment_received works normally.

  shape réponse, events retournés en list (pas en string JSON)

  events vide → 422, limite 5/compte → 429, 3 events valides acceptés, sauvegarde DB

  isolation → 404, inconnu → 404, comptage avant/après

  exception réseau → status:0, inconnu → 404, isolation → 404, inactif → 404

commit.message:
Fix marketplace RLS policies and improve error handling

FIXED:
- RLS policies were using wrong pattern (current_setting vs user lookup)
- Now checks: employees.user_id = auth.uid() and matches dealer_id
- Works with integer dealer_id type (not UUID)

IMPROVED:
- MarketplaceSettingsPage: Better error messages
- Shows specific error details instead of generic "Failed to load"
- Added validation for employee record and dealer_id

MIGRATION:
- 20260305000003_fix_marketplace_rls.sql
- Drops old policies
- Creates new policies using employees.user_id lookup pattern
- Matches pattern used by other tables (inventory, deals, etc)

TESTING:
- Tables exist and are accessible
- RLS policies now allow authenticated employees to access their dealer's data

Per-school subject name mappings and schedule filters

dot-suffixes (e.g. ETa6.ab → ETa6) for reliable lookups.

🔗 Phase-3: fix pipeline key mismatch, subscriber leak, alias bypass, reconnect safety

- coder.js: align task key namespace with TaskRunner (tasks:{pid}:{tid} instead of
  {pid}:task:{tid}:done) — fixes TCO→Deployer pipeline break
- vault-sync.js: add missing shutdown() to VaultAgent (subscriber leak fix)
- skill-zettelkasten.js: publish via canonical channel names instead of aliases
  (enables schema validation on tier-up and compound-created events)
- discord-bot.js: add _stopping guard and clearTimeout to _reconnect() so stop()
  can cleanly cancel reconnect loops

event count + token UPDATE into a single statement, and pass cached
teamConfig to waitUntil tasks to eliminate ~43 redundant D1 queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add end-to-end integration test: boat lplex -> cloud lplex-cloud -> SSE (#33)

Tests the full data path: boat Broker -> ReplicationClient -> gRPC ->
cloud ReplicationServer/InstanceManager -> cloud Broker -> HTTP SSE endpoint.

Four test cases:
- TestIntegrationBoatToCloudSSE: full round-trip with SSE stream, REST
  endpoints (/instances, /status, /devices, /values), PGN filtering
- TestIntegrationMultiBoatCloudSSE: two boats replicate to same cloud,
  each readable via its own SSE endpoint with PGN isolation
- TestIntegrationCloudSSELiveDelivery: real-time frame delivery through
  the full pipeline while SSE client is connected
- TestIntegrationCloudSSEDisconnectReconnect: boat disconnect/reconnect
  with continued SSE delivery after reconnection

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XXFCpGaMZGadRKnPAgs2xa

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace O(n^2) pg_trgm self-join with O(n) GROUP BY for duplicates

The pg_trgm self-join timed out on 35K recipes (n^2 comparisons).
Switched to GROUP BY on normalized titles which is O(n) and handles
100K+ recipes within the 30s statement_timeout. Catches all exact
and near-exact duplicates (same title, different punctuation/case).

commit.message:
feat: per-step shape for SMOD (Hold/Ramp/Smooth/ExpUp/ExpDown)

Replace global interpolation mode + per-step slide with per-step shape.
Each step independently controls its transition curve to the next value.
Right-click a step in the grid to cycle through shapes. Color-coded
indicators and transition curves drawn on each step bar.

Removes: smod.X.interp, smod.X.slideTime, smod.X.slide.Y parameters
Adds: smod.X.shape.Y (int 0-4) per-step parameters

feat: add phase-scoped MCP hints and Atlassian plugin

Simpler alternative to the reverted MCP detection engine. Adds:
- phases[] field on methodology hints for SDLC phase scoping
- 8 conditional MCP hints (Atlassian, GCP, GKE, Cloud Run, Firebase, Playwright)
- Atlassian as curated plugin (detected via existing plugin cache)
- Expanded GitHub hint triggers and phase_fit to include DESIGN/PLAN

Hints use conditional phrasing ("If X MCP tools are available...") so
Claude self-evaluates availability from its tool list. No config parsing,
no detection engine, no new schema beyond the phases[] field.
